content.claim: Hungary operates one of the most selective education systems in the EU: between-school variation in outcomes exceeds that in Austria, Germany, Lithuania, and the Netherlands (all of which also practice age-10/11 early tracking), indicating that Hungary's selection intensity is an outlier even among early-tracking countries.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: Comparison is cross-sectional and does not control for other systemic differences (e.g., curriculum design, capacity distribution) that may explain the variance difference.
This finding describes the intensity of observed selection, not the causal contribution of the 6/8-year gimnázium alone.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
